title: Correct resolution scaling without shrinking layout

testFiles.description: Tests that resize handler uses updateStyle=false and only scales via pixel ratio

tests.name: attachResizeHandler calls renderer.setSize with updateStyle=false on resize
tests.setup: Create mock renderer with spy on setSize, attach resize handler, dispatch resize event
tests.assertion: renderer.setSize was called with (width, height, false) — third argument must be false
tests.notes: The mock renderer needs to track the third argument to setSize

tests.name: attachResizeHandler calls setPixelRatio with Math.min(dpr, 2) * resolutionScale on resize
tests.setup: Set devicePixelRatio=3, create mock renderer with spy on setPixelRatio, attach with resolutionScale=0.5, dispatch resize
tests.notes: Verifies DPR capping at 2 and scale multiplication

tests.name: resize handler does not set inline style.width or style.height on canvas
tests.setup: Create renderer, attach resize handler, record canvas inline styles before resize, dispatch resize
tests.assertion: renderer.domElement.style.width and style.height are empty strings (not '100%') after resize — CSS stylesheet owns layout
tests.notes: This is the key behavioral change: JS must not touch inline CSS dimensions

tests.name: orientationchange event triggers resize handler and updates camera
tests.setup: Create renderer and camera, attach resize handler, change innerWidth/innerHeight to simulate rotation, dispatch orientationchange
tests.assertion: camera.aspect updated to new width/height ratio, camera.updateProjectionMatrix called, renderer.setSize called with new dimensions and false

tests.name: visualViewport resize event triggers resize handler
tests.setup: Mock window.visualViewport with width/height, attach resize handler, update viewport size, dispatch resize on visualViewport
tests.assertion: renderer.setSize called with visualViewport dimensions and false
tests.notes: visualViewport is preferred over innerWidth/innerHeight when available

tests.name: cleanup function removes all three event listeners (resize, orientationchange, visualViewport)
tests.setup: Attach resize handler, call cleanup, then dispatch resize, orientationchange, and visualViewport resize events
tests.assertion: renderer.setSize is not called after cleanup for any of the three event types
tests.notes: Ensures no memory leaks from stale listeners after re-attach

tests.name: resize handler with resolutionScale=1.0 on high-DPR device does not shrink canvas CSS
tests.setup: Set devicePixelRatio=3, create renderer, attach with resolutionScale=1.0, dispatch resize
tests.assertion: setPixelRatio called with 2.0 (capped), setSize called with full viewport dimensions and false, no inline CSS set
tests.notes: Verifies DPR cap does not cause layout shrinkage

tests.name: resize handler with resolutionScale=0.5 reduces pixel ratio but not CSS size
tests.setup: Set devicePixelRatio=2, attach with resolutionScale=0.5, dispatch resize at 390x844 (iPhone)
tests.assertion: setPixelRatio called with 1.0, setSize called with (390, 844, false), canvas has no inline width/height
tests.notes: Simulates mobile low-tier quality without black bars

testFiles.description: Tests that initScene calls setSize with updateStyle=false and does not set inline CSS

tests.name: initScene calls renderer.setSize with updateStyle=false
tests.setup: Mock WebGLRenderer to track setSize arguments, call initScene with container
tests.assertion: renderer.setSize was called with (window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, false)
tests.notes: The mock must capture the third boolean argument

tests.name: initScene does not set inline style.width or style.height on canvas
tests.notes: CSS stylesheet rule 'canvas { width: 100% !important; height: 100% !important }' handles layout

tests.name: initScene with resolutionScale applies it to setPixelRatio without affecting CSS
tests.setup: Set devicePixelRatio=2, call initScene with resolutionScale=0.75
tests.assertion: setPixelRatio called with 1.5 (min(2,2)*0.75), no inline CSS dimensions on canvas

tests.name: context restore re-applies pixel ratio and setSize with updateStyle=false
tests.setup: Call initScene with resolutionScale=0.5, spy on setSize and setPixelRatio, dispatch webglcontextrestored on canvas
tests.assertion: setPixelRatio called again with correct scaled value, setSize called with (innerWidth, innerHeight, false), no inline CSS set
tests.notes: Context restore must not reintroduce inline CSS dimensions

tests.name: context restore does not set inline style on canvas
tests.setup: Call initScene, dispatch webglcontextrestored, check inline styles
tests.assertion: renderer.domElement.style.width === '' and style.height === '' after context restore
tests.notes: Ensures the context loss recovery path also avoids inline CSS

testFiles.description: Integration tests verifying canvas always fills viewport with no black bars

tests.name: CSS stylesheet contains canvas rule with 100% !important for width and height
tests.notes: This is the layout guarantee — CSS owns canvas sizing, not JS

tests.name: canvas CSS dimensions fill viewport at all quality tiers (0.5, 0.75, 1.0)
tests.setup: For each resolutionScale in [0.5, 0.75, 1.0]: call initScene, check inline styles
tests.assertion: For all tiers: canvas has no inline width/height (CSS stylesheet handles it), canvas is child of container
tests.notes: Replaces T-036-04 which expected inline '100%' — now expects no inline styles at all

tests.name: iPhone SE viewport simulation: no black bars after quality scaling
tests.setup: Set innerWidth=375, innerHeight=667, devicePixelRatio=2, initScene with resolutionScale=0.5
tests.assertion: setSize called with (375, 667, false), no inline CSS, setPixelRatio=1.0
tests.notes: Simulates low-tier quality on small iPhone — canvas must still fill screen

tests.name: iPad Pro viewport simulation: no black bars after orientation change
tests.setup: Set innerWidth=1024, innerHeight=1366 (portrait iPad), initScene, attach resize, then change to 1366x1024 (landscape), dispatch orientationchange
tests.assertion: camera.aspect updated to 1366/1024, setSize called with (1366, 1024, false), no inline CSS
tests.notes: Tests iPad rotation does not introduce black bars

testFiles.description: Tests that main.ts geo callback applies quality scaling via pixel ratio only

tests.name: main.ts geo callback calls setSize with updateStyle=false when applying quality
tests.setup: Verify src/main.ts line 88 passes false as third arg to setSize
tests.assertion: renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, false) — not renderer.setSize(w, h)
tests.notes: Static analysis or mock-based test of the geo callback path

tests.name: main.ts geo callback does not set inline CSS dimensions on canvas
tests.setup: Verify src/main.ts does not contain renderer.domElement.style.width or style.height assignments after setSize
tests.assertion: No inline style assignments to canvas dimensions in the geo callback
tests.notes: Lines 89-90 in current code must be removed

existingTestsToUpdate.reason: Mock WebGLRenderer.setSize does not track the third updateStyle argument. Must update mock to capture and assert on it.
Mock WebGLRenderer.setSize does not track updateStyle arg. T-029-03 asserts canvas pixel dimensions which change when setSize gets false.
T-036-04 asserts style.width === '100%' which should now be '' (empty) since JS no longer sets inline styles. Must update assertion.

WebGLRenderer.setSize: Must track all three arguments (w, h, updateStyle) — especially the boolean third arg
WebGLRenderer.setPixelRatio: Must track calls and store the ratio for getPixelRatio
WebGLRenderer.domElement: Real HTMLCanvasElement from document.createElement('canvas') — inline styles must not be set by mock

window.visualViewport: Mock object with width, height, and addEventListener/removeEventListener
